steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Upload Accessible Feedback · Theory Upload Accessible Feedba…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 1048 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate upload byte and concurrency budgets before a quarantined state transition; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, expose preflight limits, progress, cancellation, validation details, recovery, and non-color status to keyboard and assistive-technology users.
